title: Phelline gracilior
taxonomy: Phelline gracilior belongs to the family Annonaceae. It is a species of flowering plant native to New Caledonia. The plant is part of the genus Phelline. Phelline gracilior is a rare and endangered species. Its classification also includes being a part of the order Magnoliales.
uses: Phelline gracilior, also known as the New Caledonian tree, has been traditionally used in medicine. However, there isn't much information available on its specific medicinal uses. preparations: Phelline gracilior is native to New Caledonia, where its roots are used to create herbal preparations. intro: Phelline gracilior is a plant species belonging to the family Phellinaceae, native to New Caledonia. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of various conditions such as arthritis, digestive issues, and skin problems. The bioactive constituents of Phelline gracilior include triterpenoid saponins, which have been identified as the primary active compounds responsible for the plant's medicinal properties. Phelline gracilior is often used in traditional medicine and is available in various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and extracts, which are utilized to support overall health and well-being.
